fields.description:
## Why
The DET floor is the ONLY blocking tier (every LLM criterion is advisory in v1). It must be a frozen,
deterministic, polyglot floor that fails open on any unsupported stack.

## What
- P1 readiness-shape; P2 file/symbol/import resolution (LSP via solidlsp/ast-grep); P3 package existence
  (syft + per-ecosystem registry + LLM-fallback); P4 oversize (scc/lizard); P5 task-DAG validity + interference;
  P6 AC/DD quality; P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (escalates T4). The polyglot resolution/dependency/size tooling (P2/P3/P4) is CONSUMED from the prerequisite epic 8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82 (code-grounding tooling) — this child does NOT re-build it; it owns the rebar-specific checks (P1/P5/P6/P7) + wiring P2/P3/P4 into the DET floor.

## Scope
- Wrap external tools; unsupported lang / no server / crash / timeout -> skipped = PASS (fail-open, ironclad).
- The shared tooling was extracted to prerequisite epic 8f6c (reused by code-review `9da1`); this child depends_on it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] P1-P7 implemented in Python, polyglot, fail-open (skipped=pass) tested per tool.
- [ ] Coverage recorded on the attestation.
- [ ] DET floor is the only tier that blocks.
